# LyricDisplay

> Professional real-time lyric presentation software for live events, church services, concerts and multimedia applications. Free and open-source, cross-platform desktop app built with Electron.

LyricDisplay lets operators load lyric files and instantly push selected lines to one or more transparent display outputs — designed for seamless integration with OBS Studio, vMix, Wirecast and any broadcast software that accepts browser sources. It runs a local Express/Socket.io backend that outputs are consumed from as browser URLs, enabling true transparent overlays with no additional capture hardware.

- Website: https://lyricdisplay.app
- GitHub: https://github.com/PeterAlaks/lyric-display-app
- Latest release: https://github.com/PeterAlaks/lyric-display-app/releases/latest
- Video tutorial: https://drive.google.com/file/d/1fP4fSSWSNvSocI8fK7hktdJ7dY6xnCM-/view
- Support the developers: https://buymeacoffee.com/lyricdisplay
- Developer contact: https://linktr.ee/peteralaks
- License: GPL-2.0-only
- Authors: Peter Alakembi (Lead), David Okaliwe (Co-Developer)


## Core Concepts

**Control Panel** — The main desktop window where operators load lyric files, click lines to display them live, adjust styling, and manage outputs.

**Outputs** — Independent display outputs include defaults (Output 1 and Output 2), user-created custom outputs (Output 3 through Output 6), plus a Stage output. Each is a browser-accessible URL (`http://localhost:4000/#/output1`, `/output2` ... `/output6`, `/stage`) that renders lyrics with a fully transparent background. These URLs are added as Browser Sources in OBS or Web Browser inputs in vMix.

**NDI Output** — Lyrics can also be broadcast over NDI® for direct integration into production software that supports NDI without needing a browser source.

**Secondary Controllers** — Mobile or web devices on the same network can pair with the desktop app using a 6-digit join code. Paired controllers can trigger lyric lines, toggle outputs, sync displays and submit lyric drafts for desktop approval.


## Key Features

### Multi-Output Display
- Multiple independent styled outputs (defaults + custom outputs) plus a stage display output
- Fully transparent backgrounds — perfect for browser source capture in OBS/vMix
- Real-time Socket.io synchronization across all connected displays
- NDI® output support with per-output resolution control

### Lyric Management
- Load `.txt` or `.lrc` (timestamped) lyric files
- Drag-and-drop file loading
- Built-in lyric editor (New Song Canvas) with formatting tools
- Online lyrics search from integrated providers (LRCLIB, ChartLyrics, Lyrics.ovh, Vagalume, Hymnary.org, Open Hymnal Project)
- EasyWorship song import and conversion
- Translation line support — lines wrapped in brackets `[]`, `()`, `{}` display as subtitle translations beneath the main line
- Smart auto-cleanup: removes stray punctuation, capitalizes special words (God, Jesus, etc.)

### Styling Engine
- 10 professional featured Google Fonts plus locally installed system fonts
- Typography controls: bold, italic, underline, all-caps
- Independent font color and drop shadow color/opacity
- Adjustable background color and opacity
- X/Y padding margin controls
- Position presets (e.g., Lower Third, Center)
- Full-screen mode with solid color or media background fill
- Per-output independent styling

### Workflow & Operation
- Click any lyric line to push it live to all outputs instantly
- Search bar with match highlighting and keyboard navigation (Shift+Up/Down)
- Autoplay mode for timed lyric progression
- Spacebar toggles output display on/off
- Sync Outputs button forces a manual refresh of all connected displays
- Setlist management for organizing multiple songs

### Secondary Controllers (Mobile/Web)
- Authorize devices via QR code scan or 6-digit join code
- Paired devices can: trigger lyric lines, toggle output display, run manual sync, submit lyric drafts
- Desktop app retains full approval control over submitted drafts
- Join code resets on every app restart for security

### Desktop Integration
- Auto-updates via GitHub Releases (background, seamless)
- Dark mode with system theme sync
- Full keyboard shortcut support
- Single-instance enforcement
- Native file associations for `.lrc`, `.txt`, and `.ldset` (setlist) files
- macOS open-file event handling; Windows file-launch-from-args handling
- Close confirmation dialog to prevent accidental data loss


## Supported Platforms

| Platform | Format | Notes |
|----------|--------|-------|
| Windows 10/11 (64-bit) | NSIS installer (.exe) | Run as Administrator; allow SmartScreen if prompted |
| macOS (Apple Silicon) | DMG (arm64) | Run `xattr -cr /Applications/LyricDisplay.app` before opening |
| macOS (Intel) | DMG (x64) | Same Gatekeeper workaround as above |
| Linux (x64) | AppImage | `chmod +x` then run directly |

**Minimum system requirements:** 8 GB RAM, dual-display capable GPU, 500 MB disk space, Windows 10/11 64-bit (or equivalent macOS/Linux).
**Recommended:** 16 GB RAM, dedicated GPU, dual monitors.


## Installation

### Windows
1. Download `LyricDisplay-6.3.1-Windows-Setup.exe` from the releases page.
2. Right-click → Run as Administrator.
3. If Windows SmartScreen appears, click "More info" → "Run anyway".
4. Follow the installer wizard and create a desktop shortcut.

### macOS
1. Download the appropriate `.dmg` for your chip (arm64 for Apple Silicon, x64 for Intel).
2. Open the DMG and drag LyricDisplay to Applications.
3. In Terminal, run: `xattr -cr /Applications/LyricDisplay.app`
4. Open LyricDisplay from Applications. If still blocked, go to System Preferences → Security & Privacy → General → "Open Anyway".

### Linux
1. Download `LyricDisplay-6.3.1-Linux.AppImage`.
2. Make it executable: `chmod +x LyricDisplay-*.AppImage`
3. Run it: `./LyricDisplay-*.AppImage`


## OBS Studio Integration

### Same-machine setup
1. In OBS, add a **Browser Source** to your scene.
2. Set the URL to `http://localhost:4000/#/output1` (or use `/output2` through `/output6` for additional outputs).
3. Set Width and Height to match your broadcast canvas (e.g., 1920×1080).
4. Enable "Shutdown source when not visible" and "Refresh browser when scene becomes active".
5. In LyricDisplay's settings panel, configure Output 1 styling (position, font, size, color).
6. Load a lyric file, click a line — it should appear in OBS instantly.

### Network setup (LyricDisplay on a separate PC)
1. On the LyricDisplay PC, set a static IP (e.g., `192.168.1.100`).
2. On the OBS PC, use `http://192.168.1.100:4000/#/output1` as the browser source URL (or `/output2` through `/output6` as needed).
3. If the connection fails, allow LyricDisplay through Windows Firewall on port 4000.

### vMix Integration
Same approach as OBS: Add Input -> Web Browser, use `http://localhost:4000/#/output1` (or `/output2` through `/output6`, and use the static IP for network setups), set resolution to match broadcast canvas, drag to an overlay channel.


## File Formats

| Extension | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `.txt` | Plain text lyric file, one line per lyric line |
| `.lrc` | Timestamped lyric file for autoplay sync |
| `.ldset` | LyricDisplay setlist file (multiple songs) |

**Translation line syntax:** Wrap a line in `[]`, `()`, `{}`, or `<>` to mark it as a translation/subtitle line. It will display grouped beneath the preceding lyric line.


## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Shortcut | Action |
|----------|--------|
| Ctrl/Cmd + O | Load lyrics file |
| Ctrl/Cmd + N | New song canvas (editor) |
| Ctrl/Cmd + P | Toggle autoplay |
| Ctrl/Cmd + 1 | Preview Output 1 |
| Ctrl/Cmd + 2 | Preview Output 2 |
| Ctrl/Cmd + F | Jump to search bar |
| ↑ / Numpad ↑ | Previous lyric line |
| ↓ / Numpad ↓ | Next lyric line |
| Spacebar | Toggle output display on/off |
| Shift + ↑/↓ | Navigate search results |
| Ctrl/Cmd + T | Add translation line (in editor) |
| Ctrl/Cmd + D | Duplicate line (in editor) |
| Ctrl/Cmd + L | Select line (in editor) |


## Technical Architecture

**Frontend:** React 19 with Hash Router, Tailwind CSS, Radix UI / ShadCN components, Zustand state management (with persistence), Framer Motion, Lucide React icons, Vite build tooling.

**Backend:** Express.js static file server + Socket.io WebSocket server, running on port 4000 as a Node.js child process managed by the Electron main process.

**Desktop:** Electron (cross-platform shell), electron-updater for auto-updates via GitHub Releases, electron-store for native preference persistence, Keytar for secure credential storage, native menu bridge, single-instance lock, IPC handler registration.

**Real-time communication:** Socket.io (client + server) for instant lyric line updates across all connected outputs and secondary controllers.

**Storage:** Zustand persistence for session state, SQLite (better-sqlite3) for structured data, electron-store for native app preferences.

**MIDI/OSC:** External control support via `@julusian/midi` for MIDI input integration.

**NDI:** Output via the `grandi` npm module (open-source NDI SDK wrapper). NDI® is a registered trademark of Vizrt NDI AB; LyricDisplay is not affiliated with Vizrt.


## Integrated Online Lyrics Providers

LyricDisplay does not store or redistribute lyrics. All content is fetched on-demand from public APIs for personal, church, and non-commercial use only.

- **LRCLIB** — Free synced lyrics database (~3 million songs). No API key required. Returns plain and timestamped (LRC) lyrics.
- **ChartLyrics** — Free public lyrics API. No API key required.
- **Lyrics.ovh** — Free lyrics API for educational and non-commercial use.
- **Vagalume** — Official Vagalume API (© Vagalume Media Group).
- **Hymnary.org** — Hymn texts from Hymnary.org / Christian Classics Ethereal Library (CCEL), for educational and liturgical use.
- **Open Hymnal Project** — Public domain hymn texts.


## Troubleshooting

**Browser source is black or empty:** Confirm LyricDisplay is running. Verify the URL is a valid output route such as `http://localhost:4000/#/output1` (or `/output2` through `/output6`). Click Refresh on the browser source properties. Check Windows Firewall isn't blocking port 4000.

**Network connection not working:** Confirm both PCs are on the same network. Use `http://` not `https://`. Verify the static IP is correct. Temporarily disable the firewall to isolate the issue.

**Lyrics not updating in real-time:** Click "Sync Outputs" in the settings panel. Refresh the browser source in OBS. Check that the Socket.io connection indicator (shield icon) is green in the control panel.

**macOS "damaged" or "can't be opened" error:** Run `xattr -cr /Applications/LyricDisplay.app` in Terminal before opening the app. This is expected behavior for apps without an Apple Developer code signature.

**Styling changes not applying:** Use the "Sync Outputs" button, then refresh the browser source in OBS. Check the browser console (F12) for errors.


## Development Setup

```bash
git clone https://github.com/PeterAlaks/lyric-display-app.git
cd lyric-display-app

# Install client dependencies
npm install

# Install server dependencies
cd server && npm install && cd ..

# Run in development mode (Vite + Electron)
npm run electron-dev

# Build and package
npm run electron-pack
```

Available scripts: `npm run dev` (Vite only), `npm run server` (backend only), `npm run electron-dev` (full dev), `npm run build` (production build), `npm run electron-pack` (package installer).
